target.title: fix(agents): deliver native /compact replies through source suppression
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90185 by preserving visible replies for explicit native slash commands and by keeping compaction hook messages independent from the built-in `notifyUser` compaction notices.

This PR now covers the two confirmed #90185 paths:

1. Native `/compact` fast-path replies could return before the full inline-action command marker was applied. Channels using `message_tool_only` source-reply delivery then treated the terminal command reply as ordinary source content and suppressed it, producing `queuedFinal=false, replies=0` even though compaction ran.
2. Configured compaction hook messages could suppress the built-in `notifyUser` compaction notice because the notice lived behind an `else if` branch.

## Root cause

Native slash commands can be handled before workspace bootstrap and before the normal inline command path. The full inline command path already marks terminal command replies with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression`; the native slash fast path did not. That meant an explicit `/compact` result could be generated but later dropped by source-reply delivery policy in message-tool-only channels.

The compaction notice path had a separate branch-ordering issue: hook messages and the built-in `notifyUser` notice were mutually exclusive even though they are independent user-visible lanes.

## Changes

- Mark native slash fast-path replies with the same source-suppression bypass metadata used by the full inline command path.
- Apply the marker to native slash status replies, direct command-handler replies such as `/compact`, directive replies, and inline replies returned from the fast path.
- Keep compaction hook messages and built-in `notifyUser` notices independent.
- Update the stale compaction notice comment so it matches the new behavior.
- Add a regression test proving native `/compact` command-handler replies are marked for message-tool-only delivery.
- Extend fast-path tests to assert native slash status/directive replies carry the delivery marker.
- Keep bundled Feishu `/compact` routing coverage from the earlier patch.

## Validation

Local validation on the patched branch:

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts
# Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check
# passed
```

Additional local attempts:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=verbose
# local environment reached the 180s timeout during Rolldown/Vitest build startup before assertion output

pnpm check:test-types
# local environment was stopped by SIGKILL during tsgo core test type checking
```

GitHub-hosted CI is the authoritative full validation for the pushed PR branch. For the current head, the fork CI run completed successfully and the upstream PR status checks show 0 failed and 0 pending checks.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Native `/compact` command replies and compaction notices for #90185. The command reply-loss path was `native slash fast path -> unmarked terminal reply -> message_tool_only source delivery suppression -> queuedFinal=false, replies=0`. The notice path was `hookMessages` suppressing the built-in `notifyUser` notice.

**Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw checkout for focused source validation, plus GitHub-hosted runner validation for the pushed PR branch at `b49036e4245c4a46fa2d99afdcab8bfdf8085453`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
gh run view 26957249857 --repo snowzlm/openclaw --json status,conclusion,url,headSha,jobs
pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts
git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the GitHub-hosted runner and local focused checks:

```json
{
  "status": "completed",
  "conclusion": "success",
  "url": "https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/26957249857",
  "headSha": "b49036e4245c4a46fa2d99afdcab8bfdf8085453",
  "failed": [],
  "pending": [],
  "totalJobs": 120
}
```

Upstream PR status evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "headRefOid": "b49036e4245c4a46fa2d99afdcab8bfdf8085453",
  "mergeStateStatus": "CLEAN",
  "failed": [],
  "pending": []
}
```

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check
passed
```

Relevant after-fix code path now applies the explicit command delivery marker before native slash fast-path replies leave `get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`:

```text
/compact received
native slash fast path returns the compaction reply
reply metadata is marked deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression=true
message_tool_only source delivery can deliver the explicit command feedback
```

The added regression coverage is:

```text
src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.test.ts
- marks native slash /compact command results for message_tool_only delivery (#90185)
```

**Observed result after fix:** The PR branch now has the native `/compact` fast-path reply marked for delivery before dispatch suppression policy can drop it. GitHub-hosted validation for the exact head completed successfully with no failed/pending jobs. The compaction hook/`notifyUser` branch is also independent, so hook messages no longer suppress built-in compaction notices.

**What was not tested:** A live Feishu tenant screenshot or video was not attached because no public-safe Feishu tenant transcript was available for this PR. The fix is in the shared native slash reply path and is covered by source-level regression plus GitHub-hosted validation.

## Risk / safety

- Narrow scope: only explicit native slash command replies returned by the fast path get the same delivery marker already used by the normal inline command path.
- Does not broaden ambient message delivery; the marker is applied only inside the native slash command fast path.
- Maintains existing `sendPolicy: deny` behavior because final dispatch still enforces send policy.
- Keeps room-event safeguards: dispatch still requires an explicit command turn for marked replies in room events.

## Notes

The issue references separate CardKit streaming rendering problems via related issues. This PR fixes the `/compact` reply-loss path and the compaction notice suppression path tracked here; it does not modify unrelated streaming card rendering code.
target.author: snowzlm
